key_points: Algebra helps generalize patterns using variables and expressions
Number tricks can be explained step by step using algebraic simplification
Number pyramids reveal patterns that can be written as algebraic expressions
Calendar grids show predictable numerical patterns that can be represented using variables
Digit manipulation problems can be solved by representing numbers algebraically
examples: If a number is x, then after doubling and subtracting the original number the result becomes x
Bottom row of pyramid a, b, c produces top expression a + 2b + c
Calendar grid numbers a, a+1, a+7, a+8 give sum 4a + 16
Two-digit number example: 47 can be written as 10×4 + 7
formulas: Top of 3-level number pyramid → a + 2b + c
Sum of calendar grid numbers → a + (a+1) + (a+7) + (a+8)
Difference between number and reverse → 9(b − a)
exam_tips: Convert puzzle statements into algebraic expressions
Represent unknown numbers with variables before solving
Always verify results by substituting numbers
